patterns.name: False "product unavailable" responses for existing items
patterns.description: The retrieval layer often returns an empty result set or a score below the hard‑coded threshold, causing the analyzer to reply that the item is unavailable even though the catalog contains the expected product (e.g., feeding bottles, cat sweater, shampoo, harness, cheap toy, cat bowl, specific harness ID). This happens for both single‑turn and chain‑turn queries.
patterns.fix: Lower the minimum confidence threshold and ensure the top‑N matches are always returned; if any match exists, let the analyzer propose the highest‑scoring product instead of defaulting to an "unavailable" message.

analysis.top_recommendation: Reduce the confidence cutoff in retrieval.py and modify the fallback logic so that any retrieved product (even with modest score) is suggested to the user, eliminating the blanket "no such product" reply.

selfcorrect_patch.current_text: if not results or results[0].score < CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD:
selfcorrect_patch.new_text:
if not results:
    # no matches at all – ask for clarification
    return []
# always return top‑N; let analyzer decide based on presence, not strict threshold

patch.current_text: if not results or results[0].score < CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD:
patch.new_text:
if not results:
    # no matches at all – ask for clarification
    return []
# always return top‑N; let analyzer decide based on presence, not strict threshold

final_patch.current_text: if not results or results[0].score < CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD:
final_patch.new_text:
if not results:
    # no matches at all – ask for clarification
    return []
# always return top‑N; let analyzer decide based on presence, not strict threshold
